title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE GUIMARAS DISTRICT HOSPITAL FROM ITS PRESENT TWENTY-FIVE-BED CAPACITY INTO A SEVENTY-FIVE-BED CAPACITY AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 5201 / 88 OG No. 27, 4278 (July 6, 1992)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7487, May 05, 1992 ]

AN ACT CONVERTING THE GUIMARAS DISTRICT HOSPITAL FROM ITS PRESENT TWENTY-FIVE-BED CAPACITY INTO A SEVENTY-FIVE-BED CAPACITY AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The Department of Health shall cause the conversion of the existing Guimaras District Hospital from its present twenty-five-bed capacity into a seventy-five-bed capacity hospital.

SEC. 2. The amount necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act shall be charged against the lump-sum appropriations authorized for the purpose under the current General Appropriation Act. Thereafter, such sums as may be necessary for the operation and maintenance of the hospital shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 3. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.) NEPTALI A. GONZALES

(Sgd.) RAMON V. MITRA

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on January 23, 1991 and December 3, 1991, respectively.

(Sgd.) ANACLETO D. BADOY, JR.

(Sgd.) CAMILO L. SABIO

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: May 05, 1992

(Sgd.) CORAZON C. AQUINO

President of the Philippines
